bundles.primary_trigger: a canonical layer is added, renamed, removed, or rerouted
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: public readers and machines should keep finding the same control layer without a second search step
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm discoverability and routing stay coherent across entry/control surfaces

bundles.primary_trigger: citation target, verification wording, or preferred public object changes
minimum_surfaces: affected citation-facing entry or index surfaces
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: citation drift can turn public history into a false reference map
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm citation target, verification wording, and outward download wording still describe the same object

bundles.primary_trigger: claim wording, evidentiary routing, maturity class, or claim force changes
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: a promoted claim should not outrun its status class or permitted reading force
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm canonical artifact, maturity class, and assertion strength still agree

bundles.primary_trigger: canonical home, governing source, or invariant wording changes
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: public history should not preserve a boundary shift without its governing and invariant context
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm ownership, governing precedence, and invariant wording do not contradict each other

bundles.primary_trigger: maintenance rules, acceptance standard, or promotion-state discipline changes
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: promotion discipline should not drift away from sync and acceptance discipline
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm acceptance threshold, sync obligations, and promotion-state wording still fit the same maintenance reality

bundles.primary_trigger: a stable artifact ID is added, moved, renamed, retained, or superseded
minimum_surfaces: affected maps where identity state matters
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: reference identity and supersession state should not be promoted as separate stories
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm the artifact is still the same one, or that the new state and retained trace are explicit

bundles.primary_trigger: outward handoff wording, audience path, or public-safe boundary class changes
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: outward-facing routing should not overclaim bundle safety or audience sufficiency
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm boundary class, enough-for wording, and audience path remain aligned and bounded

bundles.primary_trigger: unresolved territory, explicit non-claim, or reserved boundary wording changes
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: unresolved territory should not look settled because only one surface was promoted
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm open questions remain open and reserved territory is not silently upgraded

bundles.primary_trigger: a new package, repo, or control surface enters corpus-visible routing
minimum_surfaces: ownership, status, claims, or artifact-id surfaces as needed
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: public routing should not harden an unclassified package into canon by inertia
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm role, owner, status, and citation/reference posture are fixed before promotion

bundles.primary_trigger: touched files require integrity refresh under the current repo pattern
minimum_surfaces: only the manifests already required by the touched repo set
bundles.why_atomicity_matters: manifest churn should not become a fake substitute for canonical coherence
bundles.pre_promotion_check: confirm manifest scope stays bounded to the existing repo pattern and matches the touched surfaces

anti_patterns.problem: claims map updated without status/assertion review
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: claim routing changes while maturity class or reading force stays stale
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: public history can preserve stronger meaning than the corpus actually granted

anti_patterns.problem: new canonical layer added without routing/discoverability refresh
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: the new layer exists but control surfaces do not route readers or machines to it
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: a canonical file becomes effectively hidden and later review starts from stale entry points

anti_patterns.problem: artifact ID introduced without reference-state / supersession review
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: a new identifier appears without clear continuity or retained-state handling
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: later readers cannot tell whether promotion preserved identity or silently replaced it

anti_patterns.problem: export bundle changed without public-safe boundary review
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: outward bundle wording expands while boundary class stays unreviewed
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: public-safe drift can turn bounded handoff into accidental overexposure or overclaim

anti_patterns.problem: promotion policy changed without acceptance/sync review
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: commit-readiness language changes but local checks and sync duties do not
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: public-history promotion starts using a rule the rest of the control layer does not support

anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: canonical-home wording changes while governing-source rules stay stale
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: a weaker or non-owner surface can start behaving like override in public history

anti_patterns.problem: open-question layer changed without boundary/status review
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: unresolved territory wording moves but reserved or status surfaces do not
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: public readers may import closure or maturity that the corpus still withholds

anti_patterns.problem: manifest updated without corresponding canonical surface coherence check
anti_patterns.what_goes_wrong: hashes refresh while the logical control bundle is still inconsistent
anti_patterns.why_dangerous: integrity traces can make a broken half-promotion look more settled than it is

fail_closed_rule: If maintainers cannot determine whether a change belongs to a larger atomic bundle, promotion should pause until the minimally coupled bundle is identified.
